text.content:
The Long Dark

Setting: Neidr Aberfa (Snake Haven), Britannia

Time: Roman Britain, winter season

The investigators are invited to spend the winter at the farm of their retired friend Captio Lupis in the village of Chetelewelle. What begins as a peaceful holiday becomes a nightmare when mysterious problems plague the farm: dairy products spoil inexplicably, tools rust and warp overnight, eggs rot in hens, livestock falls ill, and people go missing. The investigators discover that the worm-like creatures of the earth, the Sprites (Little People), have awakened from underground and are causing supernatural disturbances. The situation escalates when the creatures attack during the Samhain festival, leading to a climactic battle in the woods and underground chambers where the worms have their nest.

The general setting of the adventure. A valley location with rolling hills, valleys, ancient woodlands. The valley is thickly populated with the islands only venomous reptiles (snakes). Investigators finding the ancient forests can still find the scattered remains of the ancient kingdom of the serpent people.

A small settlement where Lupis's farm lies. About two days' travel northeast of Eburacum (modern-day York). The village is built in a valley that the locals call 'Neidr Aberfa' or 'Snake Haven'. The walls of the valley rise deeply with unknown cavern systems running beneath. Farm buildings are established with careful protection from the elements.

The main setting for most of the adventure. A dairy farm operated by Lupis and his wife Carra with about 40 head of cattle. There is a large main house with workers' houses, huge barn, smokehouse, dairy building (where butter and cheese are made). Pastures for cattle take up most of the property. The farm is protected by a three-foot-tall earthen berm with a wooden tower. Each farm is between one and two miles from the nearest neighbors.

A table of nearby farms owned by retired legion members: Dobain (Auxiliary Archer), Finnbane (Auxiliary Cavalry), Matius Fronto (Legionary), Vassado (Auxiliary Scout), Espaid (Auxiliary Courier), Findgail (Auxiliary Archer), Sentius Paternus (Legionary), and Captio Lupis (Legendary). Each maintains a small armory and military readiness. Each farm is protected by a three-foot-tall earthen berm with a wooden tower.

A vast wilderness area within the valley. Investigators can find ancient ruins scattered throughout. The woods are home to various archaeological sites from the serpent people's past. These thousands of years old woods serve as a protectorium for ancient artifacts and evidence.

A gathering place for the Samhain festival celebration. A large communal bonfire is held where everyone gathers to celebrate. People drink, eat, dance, share stories, and enjoy traditions. Veterans trade old war stories and enjoy good-naturedly ribbing one another.

A deep cave system below the farm where the worms originate. Down the well of Lupis's property is a cave from which the worms emerge. They try to drive humans away. Deep in these woods, investigators can find clear, cold water that helps them establish a water source. The chamber is 4-foot deep pool of water at one end rising to dry ground.

Vast underground cavern where the worms of the earth originate. The passage leads into a vast cavern at the end of the tunnel. There is a small alcove and side passages beneath the earth to which creatures can climb out of the chamber via a 'back door' - a passage located at a steep thirty-foot-long slope. A narrow passage can only be used by creatures SIZ 12 or less, and a successful Climb check is required to reach it.
